chunks.title: Bridges, Castles & Bazaars — Setup Changes
chunks.content:
## New Tiles

Shuffle **12 new land tiles** (8 of which have the bazaar icon) into the regular tile supply.

## Per-Player Token Distribution

| Player Count | Bridges (each) | Castles (each) |
|---|---|---|
| 2–4 players | 3 | 3 |
| 5–6 players | 2 | 2 |

Each player keeps their Bridges and Castles in their personal supply alongside their followers.

chunks.title: Bridges & Castles — Quick Reference
chunks.content:
## Bridges

| Rule | Detail |
|---|---|
| When | After placing your tile (once per turn) |
| Where | On the just-placed tile OR any adjacent already-placed tile |
| What | Spans 2 opposing field edges of a tile; acts as 1 road segment |
| Scoring | 1 pt per tile — same as normal roads |
| Restrictions | Permanent once placed; cannot be placed on existing road/city/monastery segments |

## Castles

| Rule | Detail |
|---|---|
| Trigger | You complete a city of **exactly 2 tiles** |
| Action | Instead of scoring the city, place a Castle token + 1 of your followers on it |
| Fief | The 2 castle tiles + up to 2 tiles immediately left + 2 immediately right (max 6 tiles total) |
| Scoring trigger | When the NEXT road, city, or cloister completes **anywhere in the fief**, castle owner scores those same points |
| Removal | Castle and follower removed after scoring |
| Game end | Castle scores **0 pts** if no fief feature completes before end |

chunks.title: Bazaar Tiles — Quick Reference
chunks.content:
## Bazaar Tiles

| Rule | Detail |
|---|---|
| Which tiles | 8 of the 12 expansion tiles show a bazaar icon |
| Trigger | Placing a bazaar tile starts a tile auction immediately |
| Tiles drawn | Draw N face-down tiles (N = number of players) |
| Auction order | For each tile: players bid points from current score; highest bidder wins |
| Bid cost | Winner reduces their score by their bid amount |
| First bidder | The placing player bids first for each tile |
| Last tile | The placing player takes the last tile **for free** (no bid) |
| Acquired tiles | Held in front of player; may be placed on any future turn instead of drawing from supply |
| Chain auctions | Placing an acquired bazaar tile also triggers a new auction |

chunks.title: Bridges, Castles & Bazaars — Castle Option
chunks.content:
## Castle Placement (Alternative to Scoring a 2-Tile City)

**Trigger:** You complete a city consisting of **exactly 2 tiles** in Step 3.

**Normal path:** Score the city immediately (4 pts base, plus pennants).

**Castle path (optional instead):**

| Step | Action |
|---|---|
| 1 | Place a **Castle token** on the completed 2-tile city |
| 2 | Place **1 of your followers** on the castle to mark ownership |
| 3 | The city is **not scored now** — castle waits for its fief |

**Scoring the castle later:**
- When the **next** road, city, or cloister is completed **anywhere in the fief** (2 castle tiles + 2 left + 2 right, max 6 tiles), castle owner scores those points.
- Castle and follower are then removed.
- If game ends before any fief feature completes: **0 pts**.

> **Key trade-off:** You forgo the guaranteed ~4 pts from the 2-tile city, hoping a more valuable feature completes nearby.

chunks.title: Bridges, Castles & Bazaars — Bazaar Auction
chunks.content:
## Bazaar Tile Auction (Interrupts Turn)

When a tile bearing the **bazaar icon** is placed in Step 1, a tile auction begins before Step 2.

### Auction Sequence

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Draw **N tiles** from the supply face-down (N = number of players) |
| 2 | Reveal and auction each tile **one at a time** |
| 3 | The active player (who placed the bazaar tile) **bids first** on each tile |
| 4 | Players bid points from their current score; highest bidder takes the tile and reduces their score by the bid |
| 5 | The **last tile** goes to the active player **for free** (no bid) |

### After the Auction
- All N tiles are distributed. Play resumes at **Step 2** for the active player.
- Tiles won at auction are **held face-up** in front of you.

### Playing Acquired Tiles
- On any future turn, instead of drawing from the supply, you may play one of your acquired tiles as your Step 1 placement.
- If an acquired tile has a bazaar icon, it triggers another auction.

chunks.title: Bridges, Castles & Bazaars — New Scoring Rules
chunks.content:
## Castles (Fief Scoring)

A Castle replaces normal scoring for a completed 2-tile city. The 2-tile city itself scores **0 pts** when converted.

| Trigger | What Castle Owner Scores |
|---|---|
| Next road/city/cloister completed inside the fief | Same pts as that completed feature |
| Game ends before any fief feature completes | **0 pts** |

**Fief boundary:** the 2 castle tiles + up to 2 tiles to the left + 2 tiles to the right (max 6 tiles total).

**Key trade-off:** forfeiting the guaranteed ~4 pts from the 2-tile city in exchange for a potentially larger fief payout.

## Bazaar Auctions

| Event | Score Effect |
|---|---|
| You win a tile auction with a bid of N | Your score is immediately reduced by **N pts** |
| You lose an auction | No score change |

Acquired tiles have no inherent point value — only future placement scoring earns points.

## Bridges

Roads crossing bridges score **1 pt per tile** (bridge tile counts as a tile) — no change to road scoring rate.
